title: Road marking realism
desc: User feedback: white road stripes look like LED runways. ROOT CAUSE: MeshBasicMaterial { color:0xffffff } is shadeless and blows out under HDR + bloom. Fix: switch all stripe + edge meshes to MeshStandardMaterial with color 0xb8b8b8 (white) and 0xc9a23a (yellow), roughness 0.85, metalness 0, envMapIntensity 0.3. Add procedural value-noise CanvasTexture multiplied for streaky wear (256x256, repeat.set(40,1)). Replace +0.005 Y-stack with polygonOffset(-1,-1) so paint is coplanar with road and receives shadow without seam. Tier hierarchy: motorway color 0xc0c0c0, primary yellow edges, residential gets nothing. receiveShadow on. Stripe still uses emitParallelStrip dashed.
verify: Stripes no longer blow out under bloom; receive car shadow; subtle wear visible at close range.
commit: T11 road marking realism (PBR + polygonOffset + wear noise)

title: Sun + sky + exposure rebalance
desc: User feedback: sunlight too bright, too fake. ROOT CAUSE: directional 1.2 + ambient 0.15 + hemi 0.40 + bloom 0.45 threshold 0.82 + exposure 1.0 + linear fog all stack into harsh blown-out daylight with hot sun blob. Fix per research: directional 1.2->2.5, hemi 0.40->1.8, ambient 0.15->0 (delete — hemisphere covers it), toneMappingExposure 1.0->0.6, bloom strength 0.45->0.25 threshold 0.82->1.0 radius 0.55->0.6. Switch Fog (linear 60-300) to FogExp2 0.0045 with color 0xb8c8d4 (slightly warmer milky tropical). Switch shadowMap.type PCFShadowMap->VSMShadowMap, shadow.mapSize 1024->2048, shadow.radius=8, bias -0.0005. Update both day + night daynight.js presets to keep ratios consistent.
verify: Sky reads as milky hazy not deep blue; sun feels diffused not spotlight; road paint no longer glows; shadows soft.
commit: T12 sun/sky/exposure rebalance (physical units + VSM + FogExp2)

title: Tower silhouette breakup + window sophistication
desc: User feedback: buildings too fake. CBD towers are flat boxes, silhouettes too clean. Fix: each tower is now a 5-box composite (podium 4-floor wider base + main shaft + setback at 70% height inset 85% + mechanical penthouse + cylinder antenna) merged via BufferGeometryUtils.mergeGeometries -> 1 drawcall per tower. Per-tower color via material.clone(): silver/bronze/dark-blue/glass-with-brick-podium palette indexed by towerIdx % 4. Window CanvasTexture rewrite: vertical fenestration bands (3 strips, alternating spandrel-window-window-spandrel), spandrel rows every floor 30% dark band, clustered night lights via value-noise (clumps of lit floors not uniform 65%), warm tungsten color hsl(45,80%,55-65%). Tower height range expanded.
verify: From a distance towers have varied silhouettes (not identical slabs); at night windows show clusters not uniform grid; 4 distinct color schemes visible.
commit: T13 tower silhouette + window sophistication (5-box merge + variant colors)

title: Shophouse arcade + pediment
desc: Real shophouses have 5-foot-way arcade columns + pediment over roofline + air-con bracket plant-mess. Add to buildShophouses: 4 BoxGeometry(0.4,3,0.4) columns along front edge + lintel BoxGeometry(W,0.3,1) above; pediment strip BoxGeometry(W*0.6,1.2,0.3) on roof front face. Shutters baked into facade canvas texture (small dark vertical rectangles inside windows). Air-con: 2-3 InstancedMesh BoxGeometry(0.6,0.5,0.4) per shophouse on side walls.
verify: Driving Chinatown bbox shows recognizable arcade + decorative pediment + scattered AC boxes.
commit: T14 shophouse arcade columns + pediment + AC clutter

title: 3D palms (replace flat fan)
desc: Current palms.js leaf fan is 7 PlaneGeometry rectangles -> reads as flat from side angle. Replace with curved leaf strips: each leaf = 4-segment LatheGeometry or simple tapered triangle strip (start wide, taper to tip, slight droop curve). 5 leaves per palm, evenly distributed, slight stagger. Trunk stays cylinder. Keep InstancedMesh approach. If GLB available from Quaternius via direct download try that first; fallback procedural curved leaf.
verify: Palms readable as 3D from any angle; not paper-flat from side.
commit: T15 3D palm leaves (curved strips replace flat fan)
